_meta.keyKnowledge: We are learning to design, test and refine algorithms based on geometric constructions and theorems.

_source.w: We are learning to design, test and refine algorithms based on geometric constructions and theorems.
y: Designing geometric algorithms requires us to combine precise mathematical knowledge with logical, step-by-step thinking.
Testing and refining algorithms develops our debugging skills and deepens our understanding of the underlying geometry.
These skills connect geometry to computer science and are used in CAD software, robotics and computational geometry.
eg: Designing an algorithm to construct the perpendicular bisector of a line segment using compass and straightedge steps.
Writing pseudocode to determine whether a given set of three side lengths can form a valid triangle.
Creating an algorithm that tests whether a point lies inside a given circle.
Testing a bisection algorithm against edge cases and refining it to handle special configurations.
Designing an algorithm to calculate the area of any polygon given its vertex coordinates.
Comparing the efficiency of two different algorithms for the same geometric task.
